name: Matter of Time
flavour_text: Give it long enough and every minion rots to dust.
tips: 4-mana single-target removal + burn DoT + DM ramp — one of the most efficient tempo plays in the Dark toolbox once you have 4+ DM on the board.
Damage scales with your TOTAL Dark Matter pool (summed across live friendly minions), not one Mage's stacks. Build wide before casting for maximum burst.
The burn is the clutch — a partial-kill target at low HP dies at the next turn start from the burn tick. Use Matter of Time to 'finish later' when you can't quite one-shot.
Pair with [[Flame Wyrm]] — its Passive adds +1 to burn tick damage, turning Matter of Time's burn into a heavier long-tail threat.
Redundant against already-burning targets: the burn effect is a boolean, so casting a second Matter of Time on the same minion wastes the burn half. Chain onto a fresh target instead.
rulings: Damage equals the sum of Dark Matter stacks across your live minions at cast time. Zero DM on-board = 0 damage (the burn and DM grant still resolve).
Both the damage and the [[Burn]] hit the same chosen target — one target pick resolves all three effect lines.
Burn is a boolean [[Burning]] status — re-applying it to an already-burning minion is a no-op; the first burn persists until the minion dies.
The +1 Dark Matter grants to every ally Dark [[Mage]] on the board, not just the caster's lane. Applies after damage and burn resolve, so newly-banked DM does not scale THIS cast.
If the target dies to the damage, burn is applied post-mortem to a dead minion and has no effect (the grave doesn't tick).
trivia: Matter of Time is the 4-mana sibling of [[Dark Matter Barrage]] — same DM scaling, same Mage-ramp payoff, narrower target range but cheaper and carries a persistent burn.
